questions.explanation: When a customer holds a put option, the accounting depends on whether the customer has a significant economic incentive to exercise it. The repurchase price of $250,000 is below the expected market value of $300,000, so a rational customer would sell in the market rather than exercise the put, and Davenport concluded there is no significant incentive. With no significant economic incentive, the transaction is accounted for as a sale with a right of return: recognize revenue, a refund liability for expected exercises, and an asset for the right to recover the equipment as applicable. Lease accounting would apply only if the customer had a significant economic incentive to exercise (repurchase price below original price with incentive); financing treatment applies when the repurchase price is equal to or greater than the original selling price; and full deferral until expiration is not the ASC 606 model for put options lacking a significant incentive.
questions.explanationJa: 顧客がプット・オプションを保有する場合の会計処理は、顧客に行使の重大な経済的インセンティブがあるか否かで決まる。買戻価格$250,000は予想市場価値$300,000を下回るため、合理的な顧客はプット行使より市場での売却を選ぶはずであり、重大なインセンティブはないと判断されている。重大な経済的インセンティブがない場合、返品権付販売として処理し、収益の認識、予想される行使分の返金負債、回収する権利に係る資産（該当する場合）を計上する。リース処理は顧客に行使の重大なインセンティブがある場合（買戻価格が当初価格未満かつインセンティブあり）、金融取引処理は買戻価格が当初販売価格以上の場合に適用される。オプション失効まで全額繰り延べる処理は、重大なインセンティブのないプット・オプションに対するASC 606のモデルではない。

questions.subtopic: Service-Type Warranty - Allocation of Transaction Price
questions.stem: Trenholm Co. sells industrial pumps for a single contract price of $45,000 each, which includes a 4-year maintenance plan that goes well beyond assuring the pump complies with agreed specifications. The standalone selling price of the pump is $40,000 and the standalone selling price of the 4-year maintenance plan is $10,000. How much revenue should Trenholm recognize for the pump upon delivery?
questions.explanation: The maintenance plan provides a service beyond assurance that the product meets agreed specifications, so it is a service-type warranty — a separate performance obligation. The $45,000 transaction price is allocated based on relative standalone selling prices: pump = $45,000 × ($40,000 / $50,000) = $36,000; maintenance = $45,000 × ($10,000 / $50,000) = $9,000, recognized over the 4-year service period. Recognizing $45,000 at delivery ignores the separate service obligation; $40,000 uses the pump's standalone selling price without allocating the bundle discount proportionately; and $9,000 is the amount allocated to the maintenance plan, not the pump.
questions.explanationJa: このメンテナンスプランは、製品が合意された仕様に適合することの保証を超えるサービスを提供するため、サービス型保証（service-type warranty）として別個の履行義務になる。取引価格$45,000を独立販売価格の比率で配分する。ポンプ＝$45,000×($40,000/$50,000)＝$36,000、メンテナンス＝$45,000×($10,000/$50,000)＝$9,000（4年間のサービス期間にわたり認識）。納品時に$45,000全額を認識するのは別個のサービス義務の無視、$40,000はバンドル値引きを比例配分せず独立販売価格をそのまま使った誤り、$9,000はポンプではなくメンテナンスプランへの配分額である。

questions.subtopic: Loyalty Program - Allocation and Redemption-Based Recognition
questions.stem: Kestrel Stores launches a loyalty program. During Year 1, customers purchase goods for $90,000 and earn points with an estimated standalone selling price of $10,000 (after considering the likelihood of redemption). By the end of Year 1, customers have redeemed 40% of the points expected to be redeemed, and the redemption estimate is unchanged. What total revenue should Kestrel recognize for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Loyalty points provide the customer a material right and are a separate performance obligation. The $90,000 is allocated on a relative standalone selling price basis: goods = $90,000 × ($90,000 / $100,000) = $81,000, recognized at sale; points = $90,000 × ($10,000 / $100,000) = $9,000, deferred as a contract liability. Revenue on the points is recognized as they are redeemed in proportion to total expected redemptions: $9,000 × 40% = $3,600. Total Year 1 revenue = $81,000 + $3,600 = $84,600. $90,000 ignores the deferral of the material right; $81,000 omits the revenue from redeemed points; $86,400 incorrectly applies the 40% redemption rate to a 60% remaining-deferral computation ($81,000 + $5,400).
questions.explanationJa: ポイントは顧客に重要な権利（material right）を与えるため別個の履行義務である。$90,000を独立販売価格の比率で配分する。商品＝$90,000×($90,000/$100,000)＝$81,000（販売時に認識）、ポイント＝$90,000×($10,000/$100,000)＝$9,000（契約負債として繰延）。ポイント分の収益は、予想総交換数に対する実際の交換割合に応じて認識する：$9,000×40%＝$3,600。Year 1の総収益＝$81,000＋$3,600＝$84,600。$90,000は重要な権利の繰延を無視した誤り、$81,000は交換済みポイント分の収益を漏らした誤り、$86,400は40%ではなく60%相当（$81,000＋$5,400）を加算した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Exchanges for Same-Type Products Not Treated as Returns
questions.stem: Lindale Apparel sells clothing and allows customers to exchange items for the same product in a different size or color of the same quality and price. Customers may not receive cash refunds. Under ASC 606, how should Lindale account for these exchange rights at the time of sale?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record a refund liability and a return asset for expected exchanges

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize revenue in full at the time of sale; exchanges of one product for another of the same type, quality, condition, and price are not considered returns

choices.label: C
choices.text: Defer all revenue until the exchange period lapses

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reduce revenue by the estimated cost of processing expected exchanges

questions.explanation: ASC 606 specifically states that exchanges by customers of one product for another of the same type, quality, condition, and price (for example, one color or size for another) are not considered returns for accounting purposes. Because the customer cannot obtain a refund and receives an equivalent product, the entity's revenue is unaffected, so revenue is recognized in full at sale. A refund liability and return asset are required only for genuine rights of return where consideration may be refunded or credited. Deferring revenue until the exchange window closes overstates the obligation, and processing costs of exchanges are operating expenses, not a reduction of revenue.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606は、同一の種類・品質・状態・価格の他の製品との交換（色違いやサイズ違いへの交換等）は、会計上の返品とはみなされないことを明示している。顧客は返金を受けられず同等の製品を受け取るため企業の収益に影響はなく、販売時に全額収益認識する。返金負債と返品資産の計上が求められるのは、対価が返金または値引きされ得る真の返品権が存在する場合のみである。交換期間終了まで収益を繰り延べる処理は義務を過大に見積もるものであり、交換処理コストは営業費用であって収益の減額項目ではない。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Pemberton Co. delivers machinery to a customer in exchange for a single payment of $266,200 due on December 31, Year 3. The contract contains a significant financing component, and the rate that would be reflected in a separate financing transaction between Pemberton and the customer is 10%. ($266,200 / 1.10^3 = $200,000.) What amount of revenue from the sale of the machinery should Pemberton recognize on January 1, Year 1?
questions.explanation: When a contract contains a significant financing component, the transaction price is adjusted to reflect the cash selling price at the time control transfers. The present value of $266,200 discounted three years at 10% is $266,200 / 1.331 = $200,000, which is the revenue recognized on delivery. The $66,200 difference is recognized as interest income over the three years using the effective interest method ($20,000 in Year 1, $22,000 in Year 2, $24,200 in Year 3). $266,200 fails to separate the financing component; $220,000 and $242,000 are the receivable balances after one and two years of interest accretion, not the date-of-sale revenue.
questions.explanationJa: 契約に重要な金融要素が含まれる場合、取引価格は支配移転時点の現金販売価格を反映するよう調整する。$266,200を10%で3年割り引いた現在価値は$266,200÷1.331＝$200,000であり、これが引渡時に認識する収益となる。差額$66,200は実効金利法により3年間にわたり受取利息として認識する（Year 1: $20,000、Year 2: $22,000、Year 3: $24,200）。$266,200は金融要素を区分しない誤り、$220,000と$242,000は1年後・2年後の利息加算後の債権残高であり、販売時点の収益ではない。

questions.subtopic: Over Time - Reperformance Test for Simultaneous Consumption
questions.stem: Coastal Freight Co. is transporting a customer's goods from Chicago to Seattle under a single freight contract. At year-end, the shipment is halfway to its destination. In evaluating whether the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits of Coastal's performance, which analysis supports recognizing the freight revenue over time?

choices.label: A
choices.text: If another carrier took over at the midpoint, it would not need to re-transport the goods from Chicago, demonstrating that the customer has already consumed the benefit of the work performed to date

choices.label: B
choices.text: The customer pays the freight charge only upon delivery at the final destination, so the benefit is received over the transit period

choices.label: C
choices.text: The goods belong to the customer throughout transit, so any service performed on them automatically qualifies for over-time recognition

choices.label: D
choices.text: Freight services are short in duration, and ASC 606 permits over-time recognition for all contracts shorter than one year

questions.explanation: When it is difficult to assess directly whether a customer simultaneously receives and consumes benefits, ASC 606 provides a reperformance test: if another entity stepping in to complete the remaining obligation would not need to substantially re-perform the work completed to date, the customer has been consuming the benefit as the entity performs. A replacement carrier picking up the goods at the midpoint would carry them only the remaining distance — the first half of the haul would not be redone — so over-time recognition is supported. Payment timing does not determine the recognition pattern; customer ownership of the goods relates to a different over-time criterion (the entity's performance creates or enhances an asset the customer controls) and is not automatic; and there is no blanket over-time rule for contracts under one year.
questions.explanationJa: 顧客が便益を同時に受け取り消費しているかを直接判断しにくい場合、ASC 606は再履行テストを示している。すなわち、他の企業が残りの義務を引き継ぐ場合に、これまでに完了した作業を実質的にやり直す必要がないならば、顧客は企業の履行につれて便益を消費してきたことになる。中間地点で別の運送会社が引き継ぐ場合、残りの距離のみを輸送すればよく、前半の輸送をやり直す必要はないため、一定期間にわたる認識が支持される。支払時期は認識パターンを決定しない。輸送中の貨物が顧客の所有物であることは別の要件（顧客が支配する資産の創出・増価）に関する論点であり自動的に充足されるものではなく、1年未満の契約を一律に一定期間認識とするルールも存在しない。

questions.subtopic: Gift Card Breakage - Total Revenue Including Breakage
questions.stem: Avondale Retail sells $300,000 of gift cards during Year 1. Based on extensive historical experience, Avondale expects 10% of the card value ($30,000) will never be redeemed, and it is entitled to keep unredeemed amounts. By the end of Year 1, customers have redeemed $135,000 of gift cards. Using the proportional (expected-redemption) method, what total revenue should Avondale recognize from the gift card program in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Expected redemptions total $300,000 − $30,000 = $270,000. Actual redemptions of $135,000 represent 50% of expected redemptions ($135,000 / $270,000). Breakage is recognized in proportion to the pattern of redemption: $30,000 × 50% = $15,000. Total Year 1 revenue = $135,000 (redemptions) + $15,000 (breakage) = $150,000. $135,000 ignores breakage entirely; $165,000 incorrectly recognizes all $30,000 of breakage immediately; $148,500 results from applying the 10% breakage rate to the $135,000 redeemed ($13,500) instead of using the proportion of expected redemptions.
questions.explanationJa: 予想交換総額は$300,000−$30,000＝$270,000。実際の交換額$135,000は予想交換額の50%（$135,000÷$270,000）に相当する。非行使部分（breakage）は交換パターンに比例して認識する：$30,000×50%＝$15,000。Year 1の総収益＝交換分$135,000＋breakage分$15,000＝$150,000。$135,000はbreakageを全く認識しない誤り、$165,000は$30,000全額を即時認識する誤り、$148,500は予想交換額に対する比率ではなく交換額$135,000に10%を乗じた（$13,500）誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Noncash Consideration - Subsequent Fair Value Changes
questions.stem: Whitfield Consulting agrees to provide services to a customer over six months in exchange for 2,000 shares of the customer's stock. At contract inception, the shares have a fair value of $50,000. When Whitfield completes the services and receives the shares, their fair value has risen to $58,000. Under ASC 606 (as amended), how is the $8,000 increase in fair value reflected?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As additional service revenue when the shares are received

choices.label: B
choices.text: As an increase to the transaction price allocated retrospectively over the service period

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a contract asset until the shares are sold

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is excluded from revenue; revenue is measured at the $50,000 inception-date fair value, and the subsequent change is accounted for as a gain on the shares, not revenue

questions.explanation: Noncash consideration is measured at fair value at contract inception. Changes in the fair value of noncash consideration after inception that are due to the form of the consideration (such as changes in a share price) are not included in the transaction price. Revenue is therefore $50,000, and the $8,000 appreciation is accounted for under the relevant financial instruments guidance as a gain on the equity interest. Treating the increase as additional revenue or as a retrospective transaction price adjustment would improperly let market movements in the consideration's form flow through revenue, and a contract asset does not capture post-inception fair value changes of noncash consideration.
questions.explanationJa: 現金以外の対価は契約開始時点の公正価値で測定する。契約開始後の公正価値の変動のうち対価の形態に起因するもの（株価の変動等）は取引価格に含めない。したがって収益は$50,000であり、$8,000の値上がり分は金融商品に関する規定に従い株式の評価益として処理する。値上がり分を追加収益や取引価格の遡及修正として扱うと、対価の形態に係る市場変動が収益に混入してしまう。また契約資産は、契約開始後の現金以外の対価の公正価値変動を捕捉するものではない。

questions.subtopic: Royalty Exception - Scope Limited to Predominant IP Licenses
questions.stem: Ralston Co. enters into a contract to sell a customer specialized manufacturing equipment. The contract price includes a royalty of 2% of the customer's future product sales made using the equipment. The contract contains no license of intellectual property, or any license involved is clearly insignificant relative to the equipment. How should Ralston account for the sales-based royalty?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Apply the sales-based royalty exception and recognize the royalty only as the customer's sales occur

choices.label: B
choices.text: Exclude the royalty from the contract entirely because it depends on the customer's actions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Treat the royalty as variable consideration: estimate it, apply the constraint, and include the resulting amount in the transaction price at inception

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize the royalty as interest income over the expected sales period

questions.explanation: The sales- or usage-based royalty exception applies only when the royalty relates solely or predominantly to a license of intellectual property. Here the royalty relates to a sale of equipment, not predominantly to an IP license, so the exception does not apply. The royalty is ordinary variable consideration: Ralston estimates it (expected value or most likely amount), applies the constraint, and includes the estimate in the transaction price at contract inception, updating it each period. Waiting until the customer's sales occur is the exception treatment available only for predominant IP licenses; ignoring the royalty understates the transaction price; and a royalty is consideration for goods, not a financing return, so interest income treatment is inapplicable.
questions.explanationJa: 売上高・使用量ベースのロイヤルティの例外は、ロイヤルティが専らまたは主としてIPライセンスに関連する場合にのみ適用される。本問のロイヤルティは設備の販売に関連し、IPライセンスが主たる対象ではないため例外は適用されない。よって通常の変動対価として扱う。すなわち期待値法または最頻値法で見積り、制限（constraint）を適用した金額を契約開始時に取引価格へ含め、毎期見直す。顧客の売上発生まで待つ処理は主としてIPライセンスである場合の例外的取扱いであり、ロイヤルティを無視すると取引価格が過小になる。またロイヤルティは財に対する対価であって金融リターンではないため、受取利息としての処理も不適切である。

questions.stem: Under ASC 606, an entity reclassifies a contract asset to a receivable at the point when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity's right to the consideration becomes unconditional, meaning only the passage of time is required before payment is due

choices.label: B
choices.text: The customer actually pays the amount due

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entity issues its annual financial statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: The remaining performance obligations in the contract are fully satisfied

questions.explanation: A contract asset is a right to consideration that is conditioned on something other than the passage of time — typically further performance. Once the right becomes unconditional (nothing remains but waiting for the due date), the entity presents a receivable. For example, a milestone billing provision may make payment conditional on completing an additional phase; when that phase is completed and the amount is billable, the contract asset becomes a receivable. Actual cash collection extinguishes a receivable rather than creating one, financial statement issuance has no effect on classification, and full satisfaction of all performance obligations is not required — a right to a progress billing can become unconditional while other obligations remain.
questions.explanationJa: 契約資産とは、時の経過以外の条件（典型的には追加の履行）が付された対価への権利である。権利が無条件になった時点（支払期日を待つだけの状態）で債権（receivable）として表示する。例えばマイルストーン請求条項により追加フェーズの完了が支払の条件となっている場合、そのフェーズが完了し請求可能となった時点で契約資産は債権となる。現金回収は債権を消滅させるものであって債権への振替条件ではなく、財務諸表の発行は分類に影響せず、また全履行義務の充足は不要である（他の義務が残っていても、出来高請求への権利が無条件になることはあり得る）。

questions.stem: Renfrew Builders enters into a $1,200,000 fixed-price contract that qualifies for over-time recognition using the cost-to-cost input method. During Year 1, Renfrew incurs costs of $270,000 and estimates that $630,000 of additional costs will be required to complete the contract. What gross profit should Renfrew recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Total estimated costs = $270,000 incurred + $630,000 remaining = $900,000. Percentage complete = $270,000 / $900,000 = 30%. Revenue recognized = $1,200,000 × 30% = $360,000. Gross profit = $360,000 − $270,000 = $90,000. (Equivalently, total estimated gross profit of $1,200,000 − $900,000 = $300,000 × 30% = $90,000.) $300,000 is the total expected gross profit for the entire contract, not the Year 1 portion; $360,000 is Year 1 revenue, not gross profit; $75,000 results from an incorrect percentage computed as $270,000 divided by total contract price plus other errors and has no valid basis.

questions.subtopic: Discount Allocation - Observable Evidence for Specific Obligations
questions.stem: Hartwick Co. sells three distinct products in a single contract for a bundled price of $180,000. Standalone selling prices are: Product X $40,000, Product Y $60,000, and Product Z $100,000 (total $200,000). Hartwick regularly sells Products Y and Z together as a package for $140,000, and regularly sells Product X separately for $40,000. How much of the transaction price should be allocated to Product X?
questions.explanation: A discount is allocated entirely to one or more (but not all) performance obligations when the entity has observable evidence that the entire discount relates to those specific obligations: the entity regularly sells each good on a standalone basis, regularly sells a bundle of some of those goods at a discount, and that bundle discount is substantially the same as the contract's discount. The contract discount is $200,000 − $180,000 = $20,000, which equals the observable discount on the Y-and-Z package ($60,000 + $100,000 − $140,000 = $20,000). Therefore the entire discount is allocated to Y and Z, and Product X is allocated its full standalone selling price of $40,000. $36,000 results from spreading the discount proportionately across all three products ($180,000 × 40/200), which is the default method but is overridden by the observable evidence here.
questions.explanationJa: 値引きは、(1)各財を独立に定期的に販売しており、(2)その一部の財のバンドルを値引価格で定期的に販売しており、(3)そのバンドルの値引きが契約の値引きと実質的に同じである、という観察可能な証拠がある場合には、特定の履行義務のみに全額配分する。契約の値引き＝$200,000−$180,000＝$20,000であり、これはYとZのパッケージの観察可能な値引き（$60,000＋$100,000−$140,000＝$20,000）と一致する。よって値引き全額をYとZに配分し、Product Xには独立販売価格$40,000をそのまま配分する。$36,000は値引きを3製品に比例配分した結果（$180,000×40/200）であり、これは原則的方法だが本問では観察可能な証拠により上書きされる。

questions.subtopic: Principal vs Agent - Financial Statement Impact of Gross vs Net
questions.stem: Norcross Co. is evaluating whether it is a principal (gross presentation) or an agent (net presentation) for a product line. Which statement correctly describes the financial statement impact of the conclusion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Net income is higher under gross presentation because revenue is larger

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gross profit in dollars is higher under gross presentation than net presentation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reported revenue and cost of sales are both higher under gross presentation, but gross profit in dollars and net income are the same under either presentation

choices.label: D
choices.text: The choice affects only the balance sheet, not the income statement

questions.explanation: If the entity is a principal, it reports the gross amount billed to the customer as revenue and the amount paid to the supplier as cost of sales. If it is an agent, it reports only its fee or commission as revenue. The dollar amount of gross profit and net income is identical either way — only the top line and cost line differ. The conclusion still matters because revenue size affects ratios, growth metrics, and analyst evaluation. The presentation does not change profit, so claims that net income or gross profit dollars are higher under gross presentation are wrong, and the impact is on the income statement presentation, not solely the balance sheet.
questions.explanationJa: 本人（principal）であれば顧客への請求総額を収益、仕入先への支払額を売上原価として総額表示する。代理人（agent）であれば手数料部分のみを収益として純額表示する。いずれの場合も売上総利益の金額と純利益は同一であり、異なるのは収益と原価の表示額のみである。それでも本人・代理人の判定が重要なのは、収益規模が各種比率、成長指標、アナリストの評価に影響するためである。表示方法は利益を変えないため、総額表示で純利益や売上総利益額が大きくなるという記述は誤りであり、影響は貸借対照表ではなく損益計算書の表示に及ぶ。

questions.stem: Under the disclosure requirements of ASC 606, which of the following must an entity disclose about its contract balances?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Revenue recognized in the reporting period that was included in the contract liability balance at the beginning of the period

choices.label: B
choices.text: The names of customers that account for the largest contract asset balances

choices.label: C
choices.text: A forecast of contract liability balances for the next five years

choices.label: D
choices.text: The interest rate implicit in each contract asset

questions.explanation: ASC 606 requires disclosure of opening and closing balances of receivables, contract assets, and contract liabilities; revenue recognized in the period that was included in the contract liability balance at the beginning of the period; and qualitative explanations of significant changes in contract balances. This shows users how deferred amounts convert into recognized revenue. Customer names relate to concentration disclosures under other guidance (and even those do not require naming customers), forecasts of future balances are not required by ASC 606, and an implicit interest rate per contract asset is not a contract-balance disclosure requirement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606は契約残高に関して、債権・契約資産・契約負債の期首・期末残高、期首の契約負債残高に含まれていた金額のうち当期に収益認識した額、および契約残高の重要な変動についての定性的説明の開示を求めている。これにより利用者は繰延額がどのように収益へ転換するかを把握できる。顧客名は他の基準の集中リスク開示に関する論点であり（その場合も顧客名の開示自体は不要）、将来5年間の契約負債残高の予測はASC 606上要求されておらず、契約資産ごとの内在金利も契約残高の開示要求事項ではない。

questions.subtopic: Significant Financing - One-Year Practical Expedient Applied
questions.stem: On May 1, Year 1, Drummond Co. delivers custom shelving to a customer for a contract price of $80,000, with payment due on November 1, Year 1 (six months after delivery). Drummond's incremental borrowing rate is 10%, and Drummond elects all available ASC 606 practical expedients. What revenue should Drummond recognize on May 1, Year 1?
questions.explanation: ASC 606 provides a practical expedient under which an entity need not adjust the transaction price for a significant financing component if, at contract inception, it expects the period between the transfer of the good and the customer's payment to be one year or less. The gap here is six months, so the expedient applies and revenue is the full contract price of $80,000 with no interest element separated. $76,190 ($80,000 / 1.05) and $76,000 ($80,000 × 0.95) reflect unnecessary six-month discounting, and $72,727 ($80,000 / 1.10) applies a full year of discounting; all three improperly bifurcate a financing component the expedient permits the entity to ignore.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606の実務上の便法により、契約開始時点で財の移転から顧客の支払までの期間が1年以内と見込まれる場合、重要な金融要素について取引価格を調整する必要はない。本問の期間は6か月であるため便法が適用され、利息要素を区分せず契約価格$80,000全額を収益として認識する。$76,190（$80,000÷1.05）と$76,000（$80,000×0.95）は不要な6か月分の割引を行ったもの、$72,727（$80,000÷1.10）は1年分の割引を適用したものであり、いずれも便法により無視できる金融要素を不適切に区分している。

questions.subtopic: Output Method - Survey of Work Performed
questions.stem: Ashford Construction has a $400,000 fixed-price contract recognized over time. Ashford concludes that an output method based on independent engineering surveys of work completed best depicts the transfer of control. At year-end, the engineering survey certifies the project is 35% complete. Costs incurred to date are $360,000 and total estimated costs are $800,000. What revenue should Ashford recognize for the year?
questions.explanation: When an entity selects an output method, progress is measured by direct measurement of the value transferred to the customer — here, the independent engineering survey. Revenue = $400,000 × 35% = $140,000. The cost-to-cost ratio of $360,000 / $800,000 = 45%, producing $180,000, is an input measure that Ashford determined does not best depict its performance and is therefore not used. The entity applies a single method of measuring progress consistently for the performance obligation; it does not switch to whichever measure yields more revenue. $400,000 is the full contract price, appropriate only at completion, and $160,000 (40%) reflects an unsupported averaging of the two measures.
questions.explanationJa: アウトプット法を選択した場合、進捗は顧客に移転した価値の直接的測定（本問では独立した技術調査）によって測る。収益＝$400,000×35%＝$140,000。原価比率$360,000÷$800,000＝45%による$180,000はインプット法の測定値であり、Ashfordが履行を最も適切に描写しないと判断した方法のため使用しない。進捗測定方法は履行義務ごとに単一の方法を一貫して適用すべきであり、より多くの収益が出る方法に切り替えることはできない。$400,000は契約総額で完成時にのみ妥当し、$160,000（40%）は2つの測定値を平均した根拠のない数値である。

questions.stem: During Year 1, customers purchased gift cards from ABC Coffee, all of which expire on December 31 in Year 3. At the end of Year 2, some of the gift cards still have not been redeemed. How should the unredeemed gift cards be reported on ABC Coffee's year-end financial statements for Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As sales revenue.

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a current liability.

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a prepaid asset.

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an expense.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606, revenue is recognized when (or as) the performance obligation is satisfied. Gift cards represent deferred revenue — the company has received cash but has not yet delivered goods or services. At December 31, Year 2, the gift cards expire on December 31, Year 3, which is within one year of the balance sheet date, making the obligation a current liability. Revenue is recognized only when the gift card is redeemed or when it is considered unlikely to be redeemed (breakage, recognized proportionally). Until redemption or recognized breakage, unredeemed gift cards remain a current liability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606では、履行義務が充足された時点で収益を認識します。ギフトカードは前受収益を表し、会社は現金を受け取っていますが、まだ商品・サービスを提供していません。Year 2末時点で、ギフトカードはYear 3/12/31に期限が来る（貸借対照表日から1年以内）ため、流動負債に分類されます。ギフトカードが使用されるか、または使用されないと見込まれるブレイケージが認識されるまで、未使用残高は流動負債のままです。

questions.stem: Which of the following is most likely to be considered revenue, according to the Financial Accounting Standards Board's definition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A company in the business of manufacturing scientific devices recognizes a gain from the sale of agricultural land that was held for future plant expansion.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A pharmaceutical company receives cash from an insurance lawsuit settlement.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A web streaming company fulfills a 12-month service term paid by customers in advance.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A clothing reseller recognizes a gain from early extinguishment of long-term debt.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606 and the FASB conceptual framework, revenue is defined as inflows from delivering or producing goods, rendering services, or other activities that constitute the entity's ongoing major operations. A web streaming company fulfilling a 12-month service contract is earning revenue from its primary business activity. The gain from selling agricultural land (peripheral activity for a device manufacturer), an insurance settlement (non-operating), and a gain on debt extinguishment (financing activity) are all gains, not revenues — they arise from peripheral or incidental activities, not the entity's primary operations.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606およびFASBの概念フレームワークでは、収益（revenue）は企業の主要な継続的事業活動（財の引き渡し、サービスの提供等）から生じるインフローと定義されます。ウェブストリーミング会社が12ヵ月のサービス契約を履行することは主要事業から生じる収益です。農地の売却益（製造業にとって周辺的活動）・保険訴訟和解金（営業外）・負債消滅益（財務活動）はいずれも利得（gains）であり、収益ではありません。

questions.stem: On September 25, Year 1, Colson Corp. sold 200,000 widgets to Cavanaugh Corp. at $5 per unit. Half of the units were delivered on November 15, Year 1, and the remaining 100,000 units were delivered on January 20, Year 2. At the time of sale, Cavanaugh paid 40% of the contract price and agreed to pay the rest in equal installments on the two delivery dates. What amount of accrual-basis revenue should Colson recognize from this sale in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under accrual-basis accounting (ASC 606), revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied — i.e., when control of the goods transfers to the customer. Colson delivered 100,000 widgets on November 15, Year 1. Revenue for Year 1 = 100,000 units × $5 = $500,000. The remaining 100,000 units were delivered in Year 2, so $500,000 is recognized in Year 2. The cash received upfront (40% × $1,000,000 = $400,000) and the payment schedule do not determine when revenue is recognized under the accrual basis.
questions.explanationJa: 発生主義（ASC 606）では、収益は履行義務が充足された時点、すなわち商品の支配が顧客に移転したときに認識する。Colsonは Year 1の11月15日に100,000ウィジェットを引き渡したため、Year 1の収益 = 100,000単位 × $5 = $500,000。残りの100,000単位はYear 2に引き渡されるため$500,000はYear 2に認識する。前受金はYear 1の収益認識額に影響しない。

questions.stem: A shoe retailer allows customers to return shoes within 90 days of purchase. The company estimates that 5% of sales will be returned within the 90-day period. During the month, the company has sales of $200,000 and returns of sales made in prior months of $5,000. What amount should the company record as net sales revenue for new sales made during the month?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606, variable consideration such as estimated returns must be excluded from the transaction price. For the $200,000 of new sales, the estimated return rate is 5%, so estimated returns = $200,000 × 5% = $10,000. Net sales revenue for new sales = $200,000 − $10,000 = $190,000. The $5,000 of actual returns from prior months relate to revenue previously recognized in prior periods and do not affect the current month's revenue from new sales.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606では、返品見積りなどの変動対価は取引価格から除外する。今月の新規売上$200,000のうち、返品見積額 = $200,000 × 5% = $10,000を控除する。新規売上の純収益 = $200,000 − $10,000 = $190,000。過去月の実際返品$5,000は過去期間に認識済みの収益に対応するため、今月の新規売上収益には影響しない。

questions.stem: During the current year, Casual Wear Co. had total retail sales of $800,000 and collected a 5% state sales tax on all sales. At the end of the prior year, Casual Wear had $4,500 in sales taxes that had not been remitted to the state authorities. During the current year, Casual Wear remitted $39,500 in state sales tax. What amount should be recorded in Casual Wear's current-year financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $5,000 in sales tax payable.

choices.label: B
choices.text: $39,500 in sales tax expense.

choices.label: C
choices.text: $40,000 in sales tax revenue.

choices.label: D
choices.text: $840,000 in sales revenue.

questions.explanation: Sales taxes collected from customers are not revenue for the retailer — they are a liability owed to the government. Sales tax collected during the current year = $800,000 × 5% = $40,000. Beginning sales tax payable = $4,500. Remitted = $39,500. Ending sales tax payable = $4,500 + $40,000 − $39,500 = $5,000. Therefore, $5,000 should be reported as sales tax payable (a current liability) at year-end.
questions.explanationJa: 顧客から収集した消費税は小売業者の収益ではなく、州に納付すべき負債である。当期収集消費税 = $800,000 × 5% = $40,000。前期末未払消費税$4,500 + 当期収集$40,000 − 当期納付$39,500 = $5,000が期末未払消費税（流動負債）として計上される。

questions.subtopic: Over-Time Revenue Recognition — Contract Asset vs. Liability
questions.stem: Falton Co. had the following first - year amounts related to its $9,000,000 construction contract: Actual costs incurred and paid $2,000,000 Estimated costs to complete 6,000,000 Progress billings 1,800,000 Cash collected 1,500,000 Falton Co. accounts for revenue from the contract as the performance obligation is being satisfied and measures progress on the contract based on costs incurred. What amount, if any, should Falton recognize as a current liability at year end?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-45-3, a contract liability arises only when billings exceed revenue recognized to date. Total estimated cost = $2,000,000 + $6,000,000 = $8,000,000; percentage complete = $2,000,000 / $8,000,000 = 25%. Revenue recognized = 25% × $9,000,000 = $2,250,000. Since billings of $1,800,000 are less than revenue recognized of $2,250,000, Falton has a contract asset (not a liability) of $450,000. Therefore, the current liability is $0.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-45-3により、契約負債は請求額が認識済み収益を超える場合にのみ生じる。総見積原価は$2,000,000＋$6,000,000＝$8,000,000、進捗率は$2,000,000÷$8,000,000＝25%。認識収益は25%×$9,000,000＝$2,250,000。請求額$1,800,000は認識収益$2,250,000を下回るため、Faltonは契約資産（負債ではない）$450,000を計上する。したがって流動負債は$0である。

questions.stem: Haft Construction Co. has consistently recognized income from long-term contracts as its construction obligations are being satisfied. On January 10, Year 1, Haft began work on a $3,000,000 construction contract to be completed in three years. The following data relate to the progress of the contract as of December 31, Year 2: Income recognized at 12/31/Year 1 $300,000 Costs incurred 1/10/Year 1 through 12/31/Year 2 1,800,000 Estimated cost to complete 600,000 If Haft measures progress toward completion of its contract obligations using the cost-to-cost approach, what amount of gross profit should Haft report in its income statement for the year ended December 31, Year 2?
questions.explanation: Total estimated cost = $1,800,000 + $600,000 = $2,400,000. Total estimated profit = $3,000,000 − $2,400,000 = $600,000. Percentage complete through Year 2 = $1,800,000 / $2,400,000 = 75%. Cumulative profit earned through Year 2 = 75% × $600,000 = $450,000. Year 2 gross profit = $450,000 cumulative − $300,000 recognized in Year 1 = $150,000 (ASC 606-10-25-27).

questions.stem: PJ Paint & Body sells car parts and provides services. PJ is contracted to provide ten replacement bumpers to Smyth New & Used Auto Mall. When should PJ recognize revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When all the bumpers are delivered to Smyth.

choices.label: B
choices.text: As soon as the purchase p rice is collected.

choices.label: C
choices.text: As each bumper is delivered to Smyth.

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the contract is signed.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-19, each bumper is a distinct good; control transfers when each unit is delivered. Because each bumper can be used independently and is separately identifiable, there are ten separate performance obligations. Revenue is recognized as each bumper is delivered, not when the entire order is complete or when the contract is signed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-19により、各バンパーは別個の財であり、それぞれが納品された時点でコントロールが移転する。各バンパーは独立して使用でき、個別に識別可能であるため、10件の独立した履行義務が存在する。収益は契約締結時や全量納品時ではなく、各バンパーの納品時点で認識する。

questions.stem:
ABC Co. is working on a construction contract. They have been recognizing costs and revenues from the contract as the performance obligation is being satisfied. The contract was started in Year 5 and was completed in Year 7 for a price of $9,000,000. The following cost information was provided:

| | December 31, Year 5 | December 31, Year 6 |
|---|---|---|
| Cumulative contract costs incurred | $3,900,000 | $6,000,000 |
| Estimated total cost at completion | $7,800,000 | $8,000,000 |

If ABC measures progress on the contract based on costs incurred, how much profit should the company recognize for the year ended December 31, Year 6?
Which of the following is a part of the core principle of the revenue recognition standard?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The amount of revenue recognized represents the consideration the entity expects to receive in exchange for goods or services.

choices.label: B
choices.text: If the expected cost of satisfying a performance obligation is greater than the amount of revenue allocated to it, the loss will be recognized when the cost is incurred.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recoverable incremental costs of obtaining a contract are capitalized and reported on the income statement in the same period in which the related revenue is recognized.

choices.label: D
choices.text: To recognize revenues while the performance obligation is being satisfied, there must be some means for measuring progress toward completion of the obligation.

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-10-2 states the core principle: an entity recognizes revenue to depict the transfer of promised goods or services in an amount that reflects the consideration to which the entity expects to be entitled in exchange for those goods or services. The other choices describe supplementary rules (onerous contract treatment, cost capitalization, and progress measurement) that are not part of the core principle itself.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-10-2のコア原則：企業は、財またはサービスの顧客への移転を、交換に受け取ると見込まれる対価の額で描写するように収益を認識する。他の選択肢はそれぞれ、損失契約の処理、契約獲得コストの資本化、進捗測定の要件といった補足的なルールであり、コア原則そのものではない。

questions.subtopic: Revenue Recognition at Point of Delivery
questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, Acme Fuel Co. sold 100,000 gallons of heating oil to Karn Co. at $3 per gallon. Fifty thousand gallons were delivered on December 15, Year 1, and the remaining 50,000 gallons were delivered on January 15, Year 2. Payment terms were: 50% due on October 1, Year 1, 25% due on first delivery, and the remaining 25% due on second delivery. What amount of revenue should Acme recognize from this sale during Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-30, revenue is recognized when control of the goods transfers to the customer, not when payment is received. Only 50,000 gallons were delivered in Year 1 (December 15). Revenue = 50,000 × $3 = $150,000. The October 1 cash receipt of 50% ($150,000) is deferred as a contract liability until delivery occurs; the Year 2 delivery will trigger the remaining $150,000 of revenue.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-30により、収益は代金受領時ではなく顧客へのコントロール移転時に認識する。第1年度に納品されたのは50,000ガロン（12月15日）のみ。収益＝50,000×$3＝$150,000。10月1日の前受金50%（$150,000）は納品まで契約負債として繰り延べ、第2年度の納品時に残り$150,000の収益を認識する。

questions.subtopic: Right of Return — Journal Entry Components
questions.stem: Baxley Co. makes a sale on account to a longtime customer on December 31, Year 5, for $3,000. The customer has 90 days to return the product if they are not satisfied with it. Based on prior experience, Baxley has determined that approximately 15% of similar products are returned within the acceptable 90-day period. The journal entry to record the sale on December 31, Year 5, should include which of the following?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A $3,000 debit to accounts receivable.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A $450 debit to right to recover goods.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A $3,000 credit to sales revenue.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A $2,550 credit to refund liability.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-23, when a right of return exists, the seller records: (1) revenue for the expected non-returned portion ($3,000 × 85% = $2,550 credit to sales revenue); (2) a refund liability for the expected returns ($3,000 × 15% = $450 credit); and (3) a right-to-recover-goods asset at the cost of the goods expected to be returned (debit). A $450 debit to right to recover goods is a correct element of the journal entry, representing the expected cost of goods to be returned. The accounts receivable debit should be $3,000 (the full amount owed), but the revenue credit is only $2,550, not $3,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-23により、返品権がある場合の仕訳は次のとおり。①返品されないと見込まれる部分の収益（$3,000×85%＝$2,550貸方）、②返品見込み分の返金負債（$3,000×15%＝$450貸方）、③返品予定商品の回収権（原価で借方）。選択肢Bの$450借方（回収権）は、仕訳の正しい構成要素である。売掛金の借方は全額$3,000が正しく、$3,000の売上収益は不正確（$2,550が正しい）。

questions.subtopic: Right of Return — Revenue Deferral When Returns Cannot Be Estimated
questions.stem: On May 1, Clothes Co. sells clothing to Link Corp. for $40,000. Clothes ships the clothing on May 1 and Link is obligated to pay Clothes within six months. Link is given 12 months to return any of the clothing for a refund if they experience low demand. Link is also given 18 months to exchange any clothing due to low demand. At the time of sale, Clothes cannot reasonably estimate returns, but estimates $5,000 in exchanged goods. Clothes should recognize revenue for the aforementioned transaction

choices.label: A
choices.text: On the day of the sale.

choices.label: B
choices.text: 6 months after the date of sale.

choices.label: C
choices.text: 12 months after the date of sale.

choices.label: D
choices.text: 18 months after the date of sale.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-22, if an entity cannot reasonably estimate returns, it cannot recognize revenue until the return period expires or estimation becomes possible. Since Clothes cannot estimate refund returns, revenue must be deferred until the 12-month return (refund) period expires. The 18-month exchange period does not preclude revenue recognition because exchanges for equal or greater value do not reduce revenue; they are already accounted for through the $5,000 estimate.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-22により、返品額を合理的に見積もれない場合、返品期間が終了するまで収益を認識できない。Clothesは返金返品を見積もれないため、12か月の返品（返金）期間が終了するまで収益を繰り延べる必要がある。18か月の交換期間は収益認識を妨げない（同等以上の価値との交換は収益を減少させず、$5,000の見積もりで処理済み）。

questions.subtopic: Over-Time vs. Point-in-Time Revenue Recognition Criteria
questions.stem: An entity has agreed to a three-year contract with a customer. Recognizing revenue from long-term contracts over a period of time is not required when

choices.label: A
choices.text: The customer consumes the performance obligation as it is being delivered.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The customer has direct use of the performance obligation.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The customer has control over the performance obligation while it is being created.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entity has no alternative use for the performance obligation.

questions.explanation: ASC 606-10-25-27 specifies three criteria for over-time recognition, any one of which is sufficient: (a) the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits, (b) the entity creates or enhances an asset that the customer controls as it is created, or (c) the entity's performance does not create an asset with alternative use to the entity AND the entity has an enforceable right to payment for performance completed to date. Having no alternative use for the asset alone (without the accompanying right to payment) does not by itself trigger over-time recognition.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-27は、一定期間にわたる収益認識の条件を3つ定めている（いずれか1つを満たせばよい）：(a)顧客が便益を受け取りながら同時に消費する、(b)顧客がコントロールする資産を創出・強化する、(c)他の用途に転用できない資産であり、かつ完了済み履行に対する支払請求権が存在する。「他の用途への転用不能」だけでは（支払請求権なしでは）一定期間にわたる認識は要求されない。

questions.stem: Red Co. has a long-term construction contract that qualifies for recognition of revenue while the performance obligations are being met. Red will use costs incurred as the measure of progress toward completion of the project. In determining the amount of profit to recognize for the second year, which of the following items would Red not use in this calculation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Previously recognized profit.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Total estimated profit.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Progress bi llings to date.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Total estimated cost.

questions.explanation: Under the cost-to-cost method per ASC 606-10-25-23, Year 2 profit = (cumulative costs incurred / total estimated costs) × total estimated profit − previously recognized profit. The calculation requires: total estimated profit, total estimated cost, and previously recognized profit. Progress billings are used only for balance sheet presentation (to determine contract asset or liability) and have no role in computing profit recognized in a given period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-23のコスト法では、第2年度利益＝（累計発生原価÷総見積原価）×総見積利益－既認識利益。この計算に必要なのは総見積利益、総見積原価、既認識利益の3つ。工事請求額（進捗請求）は、契約資産・負債の貸借対照表表示にのみ使用され、期間利益の算定には関係しない。

questions.stem: Green Co. has two long-term construction contracts. One contract, with Blue Co., qualifies for revenue recognition while the performance obligation is being satisfied. The other contract, with Red Co., does not qualify for revenue recognition until the performance obligation is fully satisfied. For either of these two contracts, what account would be debited when preparing the journal entry to record billings? Blue Co. Red Co.

choices.label: A
choices.text: Billings Cash

choices.label: B
choices.text: Construction receivable Construction receivable

choices.label: C
choices.text: Cash Billings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Construction in progress Construction in progress

questions.explanation: When a contractor sends a billing (invoice) to a customer, the entry is: debit Accounts Receivable (or Construction Receivable) and credit the appropriate revenue or billings account. This applies to both over-time contracts (Blue Co.) and point-in-time contracts (Red Co.). Under the over-time method, the credit is to revenue; under the point-in-time method, the credit is to Billings on Construction in Progress. In both cases the debit is to Construction Receivable.
questions.explanationJa: 請負業者が顧客に請求書を発行した際の仕訳は、借方：売掛金（建設工事未収入金）、貸方：収益または工事請求額（契約形態による）。一定期間認識契約（Blue Co.）では貸方が収益、完成時点認識契約（Red Co.）では貸方が工事請求額。どちらの場合も借方は建設工事未収入金（Construction Receivable）となる。

questions.stem: An auto repair company has agreed to provide a customer with a package of standard car maintenance services for $100. The package consists of an oil change, for which the company normally charges $75, and a tire rotation, for which the company normally charges $50. What amount of revenue should be recognized from the tire rotation?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-31, the transaction price is allocated to each distinct performance obligation based on relative standalone selling prices. Total standalone selling prices = $75 (oil change) + $50 (tire rotation) = $125. The tire rotation's share = $50 / $125 = 40%. Revenue allocated to tire rotation = 40% × $100 = $40.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-31により、取引価格は独立販売価格の比率で各履行義務に配分する。独立販売価格合計＝$75（オイル交換）＋$50（タイヤローテーション）＝$125。タイヤローテーションの比率＝$50÷$125＝40%。タイヤローテーション配分額＝40%×$100＝$40。

questions.stem: On March 1, Bunyan Lumber entered into a contract to provide 20,000 feet of Brazilian cherry to Happy Homes, LLC, for $15 per foot, as requested from July to October. At the time, Bunyan could purchase Brazilian cherry for $10 per foot. The following events occurred after the contract was signed: In April, foresters identified a new disease affecting Brazilian cherry. In May, regulations were implemented that require transport inspections of Brazilian cherry. Due to the inspections and decreased supply, prices increased and fluctuated between $13 and $21 per foot. In June, Bunyan purchased 20,000 feet of Brazilian cherry for $16 per foot. Bunyan delivered 50% of the lumber in August and 50% in September. When should Bunyan recognize a loss related to this contract?

choices.label: A
choices.text: May.

choices.label: B
choices.text: June.

choices.label: C
choices.text: August.

choices.label: D
choices.text: August and September.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-7, when a contract becomes onerous — meaning total expected costs exceed total contract revenues — the expected loss must be recognized immediately. In May, prices fluctuated between $13 and $21; because the price could still be below the $15 contract price, a definitive loss was not yet determinable. In June, Bunyan actually purchased at $16 per foot, which is $1 above the $15 contract price, making the loss of $20,000 (20,000 × $1) certain and measurable. The full loss should be recognized in June.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-7により、契約が損失契約になった（総見積費用が総契約収益を超える）時点で損失を即時認識する。5月は価格が$13〜$21と変動しており、損失が確定していなかった。6月にBunyanが$16/フィートで購入した時点で、$15の契約価格を$1超過することが確定し、$20,000（20,000×$1）の損失が確定・測定可能となる。損失は6月に認識すべきである。

questions.subtopic: Relative Standalone Selling Price Allocation — Multi-Component Bundle
questions.stem: Mod Cons Appliances entered into a contract to provide Reilly Clean Laundromat with 10 premium washer-dryer packages at $2,150 per package. The premium washer-dryer package includes a washing machine, a dryer, and a 5-year repair plan for each appliance. Mod Cons offers a washer package with a washer and a washer repair plan for $1,350 and a dryer package with a dryer and a dryer repair plan for $950. Mod Cons regularly sells individual washing machines at $1,200 each and individual dryers at $900 each. It sells 5-year repair plans at $300 for washing machines and $100 for dryers. Rounding to the nearest $100, how much of the Reilly contract revenue should be allocated to washing machines?
questions.explanation: Because the contract bundles two smaller bundles (a washer package and a dryer package) that each carry their own embedded discount, ASC 606-10-32-31 requires a step allocation: first allocate each smaller bundle's discount among its own components, then allocate any residual discount across all four performance obligations based on their discount-adjusted standalone prices. Washer package: standalone total $1,200 + $300 = $1,500 vs. bundle price $1,350, a $150 discount allocated 80%/20%, giving adjusted standalone prices of washer $1,080 and washer repair plan $270. Dryer package: standalone total $900 + $100 = $1,000 vs. bundle price $950, a $50 discount allocated 90%/10%, giving adjusted standalone prices of dryer $855 and dryer repair plan $95. The four adjusted standalone prices sum to $2,300 versus the actual package price of $2,150, leaving a further $150 discount allocated proportionally: washer allocation = $2,150 × ($1,080 / $2,300) ≈ $1,009.57 per package. For 10 packages: $1,009.57 × 10 ≈ $10,095.65, which rounds to the nearest $100 as $10,100.
questions.explanationJa: この契約は、それぞれ独自の値引きを内包する2つの小さいバンドル（washer packageとdryer package）を含むため、ASC 606-10-32-31に基づき段階配分が必要である。まず各小バンドルの値引きをその構成要素に配分し、その後残りの値引きを調整後独立価格の比率で全4履行義務に配分する。washer package：独立価格合計$1,200＋$300＝$1,500に対しバンドル価格$1,350、値引き$150を80%／20%で配分し、調整後独立価格は洗濯機$1,080、洗濯機修理プラン$270となる。dryer package：独立価格合計$900＋$100＝$1,000に対しバンドル価格$950、値引き$50を90%／10%で配分し、調整後独立価格は乾燥機$855、乾燥機修理プラン$95となる。4項目の調整後独立価格の合計は$2,300で、実際のパッケージ価格$2,150との差額$150をさらに比例配分する。洗濯機への配分＝$2,150×($1,080/$2,300)≈$1,009.57／パッケージ。10パッケージでは$1,009.57×10≈$10,095.65となり、$100未満四捨五入で$10,100となる。

questions.subtopic: Transaction Price Allocation — Bundled Products and Services
questions.stem: Niwot Co. sells products and service plans both separately and bundled together. Willy Loman, a Niwot Co. salesman, sold a Widgetron in 2014 for $1,000, its normal price, and told the customer he'd 'throw in the 3- year service plan for free', which the company normally sells for an additional $150. The product shipped, the customer paid, and $1,000 in revenue was recognized in 2014. Is this correct? Why or why not?

choices.label: A
choices.text: This is correct because the understanding between the seller and customer was that the product price was $1,000 and the service plan was free, which is in accordance with the principle of faithful representation.

choices.label: B
choices.text: This is incorrect because services must be rendered before any revenue can be recognized.

choices.label: C
choices.text: This is correct because the product shipped and the customer paid.

choices.label: D
choices.text: This is incorrect because the services are not truly free and service revenue must be allocated over the three years of the service plan.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-32-31, the transaction price of $1,000 must be allocated among all distinct performance obligations based on relative standalone selling prices. The product ($1,000 standalone) and the 3-year service plan ($150 standalone) are both distinct obligations. Allocated revenue: product = $1,000/$1,150 × $1,000 ≈ $870; service = $150/$1,150 × $1,000 ≈ $130, recognized ratably over three years. Recognizing the full $1,000 in 2014 overstates product revenue and omits the service revenue deferral required under ASC 606.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-32-31により、$1,000の取引価格は独立販売価格の比率ですべての履行義務に配分する必要がある。製品（独立価格$1,000）と3年間サービスプラン（独立価格$150）はいずれも別個の履行義務である。配分：製品≈$870（2014年認識）、サービス≈$130（3年間で均等認識）。$1,000を全額2014年に認識することは製品収益を過大計上し、サービス収益の繰り延べを省略するため誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Anticipated Loss Recognition on Long-Term Contracts
questions.stem: When should an anticipated loss from a long-term contract be recognized if revenue from the contract is recognized over a period of time and at a point in time, respectively? Over a period of time At a point in time

choices.label: A
choices.text: Over the life of the project When the project is complete

choices.label: B
choices.text: Immediately When the project is complete

choices.label: C
choices.text: Over the life of the project Immediately

choices.label: D
choices.text: Immediately Immediately

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-7, regardless of whether the performance obligation is satisfied over time or at a point in time, an anticipated loss on a contract must be recognized in full immediately when it becomes probable. This conservatism principle prevents deferring expected losses. There is no exception based on the revenue recognition method; both over-time and point-in-time contracts must recognize the full loss when the loss becomes foreseeable.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-7により、履行義務が一定期間にわたって充足されるか特定の時点で充足されるかにかかわらず、契約上の損失が見込まれる場合には即時に全額を認識しなければならない。この保守主義の原則により、損失の繰り延べは認められない。収益認識の方法による例外はなく、一定期間認識・特定時点認識のいずれの契約も、損失が予見可能になった時点で全額認識する。

questions.subtopic: Contract Modifications — New Contract vs. Modification of Existing Contract
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a furniture wholesale company agreed to sell 10 identical couches to a customer at a price of $1,000 per couch. The entity transfers control of each couch at the point in time it is delivered to the customer. By July 1, Year 1, 6 couches had been provided to the customer. On that date, the customer offered to purchase 2 additional couches of the same model at a price of $950 per couch. If the company agrees to accommodate the customer's request, does the modification in the order constitute a new contract?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, because the amount of consideration offered is not a material change from the price in the original contract.

choices.label: B
choices.text: No, because the modification includes additional couches that are identical to those in the original contract.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes, because the modification was requested within one year of the original contract.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, because the modification includes additional couches that are distinct from those in the original contract.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-12, a modification is treated as a new separate contract only if (1) the additional goods or services are distinct AND (2) the price of the additional goods reflects their standalone selling price. The additional couches are identical to the originals (distinct), but the offered price of $950 is below the $1,000 standalone selling price. Because condition (2) is not met, the modification is not a new contract; it is accounted for as a modification of the existing contract (prospectively, given the remaining couches are distinct). The most factually supported 'No' answer is that the couches are identical to those already in the contract, not a separate transaction at standalone pricing.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-12により、契約変更が新たな独立契約として処理されるには、①追加財・サービスが別個のものであること、②追加財・サービスの価格が独立販売価格を反映していること、の両方を満たす必要がある。追加ソファは元と同一（別個）だが、提示価格$950は独立販売価格$1,000を下回るため、条件②を満たさない。したがって新契約とはならず、既存契約の変更として処理する（残余ソファが別個であることから将来的に処理）。

questions.subtopic: Revenue Recognition — Collectability and Consignment-Like Arrangements
questions.stem: On December 30, Devlin Co. sold goods to Jensen Co. for $10,000, under an arrangement in which (1) Jensen has an unlimited right of return and (2) Jensen's obligation to pay Devlin is contingent upon Jensen's reselling the goods. Past experience has shown that Jensen ordinarily resells 60% of goods and returns the other 40%. What amount should Devlin include in sales revenue for this transaction on its December 31 income statement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-1, one of the criteria for revenue recognition is that collection of the consideration is probable. Here, Jensen's obligation to pay is contingent upon resale, meaning collection is not unconditional (or probable) at the time of sale. When the buyer's payment obligation is contingent on reselling to a third party, the arrangement resembles a consignment; revenue is recognized only when Jensen resells the goods. Devlin should recognize $0 on December 31.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-1により、収益認識の要件の一つは対価の回収可能性が高いことである。本件ではJensenの支払義務が転売を条件としているため、売却時点での回収可能性は無条件でなく（高くない）。買主の支払義務が第三者への転売を条件とする場合、委託販売に類似した取引となり、Jensenが転売した時点でのみ収益を認識する。したがって12月31日は$0の認識となる。

questions.stem: During 20X2, Fleet Co.'s trademark was licensed to Hitch Corp. for royalties of 10% of net sales of the trademarked items. Returns were estimated to be 1% of gross sales. On signing the licensing agreement, Hitch paid Fleet $75,000 as an advance against future royalty earnings. Gross sales of the trademarked items during the year were $600,000. What amount should Fleet report as royalty income for 20X2?
questions.explanation: Royalties are based on net sales. Net sales = $600,000 × (1 − 1%) = $594,000. Royalty income = 10% × $594,000 = $59,400. The $75,000 advance is treated as a prepayment (contract liability) and offset against earned royalties; it does not represent additional income beyond the $59,400 earned. Fleet reports $59,400 in royalty income for 20X2 (ASC 606-10-55-65).
questions.explanationJa: ロイヤルティは純売上に基づいて計算する。純売上＝$600,000×(1－1%)＝$594,000。ロイヤルティ収入＝10%×$594,000＝$59,400。$75,000の前払いは前受金（契約負債）として処理し、稼得したロイヤルティと相殺する。前払い額が稼得分を超える部分は追加収益とならない。20X2年のロイヤルティ収入は$59,400（ASC 606-10-55-65）。

questions.stem: A company produces three products. Normal pricing information on the products is as follows: Product A $80 Product B 20 Product C 60 The company regularly offers a discounted price of $90 when Products A and B are purchased together. If the company contracts to sell a package including all three products for a discounted price of $100, what amount of the discount should be allocated to Product A?
questions.explanation: Total standalone selling prices = $80 + $20 + $60 = $160; total contract discount = $160 − $100 = $60. Because the company regularly sells A and B together for $90 (a $10 discount from their combined $100 standalone price), ASC 606-10-32-37 requires that observable $10 discount to be allocated only to A and B, based on their relative standalone selling prices: A = $80/$100 × $10 = $8; B = $20/$100 × $10 = $2. This leaves adjusted standalone prices of A = $72, B = $18, and C = $60 (total $150 = $90 bundle + $60 for C). The remaining discount of $50 ($150 − $100) is then allocated across all three products based on these adjusted standalone prices: A = $72/$150 × $50 = $24; B = $18/$150 × $50 = $6; C = $60/$150 × $50 = $20. Total discount allocated to Product A = $8 + $24 = $32.
questions.explanationJa: 独立販売価格合計＝$80＋$20＋$60＝$160。総割引額＝$160－$100＝$60。A・Bを合わせて$90で販売するのが通例（$100の独立販売価格合計から$10の割引）であるため、ASC 606-10-32-37に従い、この観察可能な$10の割引はA・Bの独立販売価格の比率で両者にのみ配分する：A＝$80÷$100×$10＝$8、B＝$20÷$100×$10＝$2。この結果、調整後独立販売価格はA＝$72、B＝$18、C＝$60（合計$150＝バンドル$90＋C$60）となる。残りの割引$50（$150－$100）は、この調整後独立販売価格の比率で3商品全てに配分する：A＝$72÷$150×$50＝$24、B＝$18÷$150×$50＝$6、C＝$60÷$150×$50＝$20。Product Aへの割引合計＝$8＋$24＝$32。

questions.subtopic: Point-in-Time Revenue Recognition — Completed Contract Method
questions.stem: A company accounts for a long-term construction contract by recognizing income and expenses at a point in time. Revenue is recognized when: (1) Cash is collected and (2) Progress billings exceed recorded costs?

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Yes, (2) Yes

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) Yes, (2) No

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) No, (2) Yes

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-30, for point-in-time recognition on construction contracts, revenue is recognized when control transfers to the customer upon contract completion, not when cash is collected and not when progress billings exceed recorded costs. Cash collection and billing milestones are factors in the balance sheet presentation of contract assets and liabilities, but neither triggers revenue recognition under the completed-contract (point-in-time) approach.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-30により、特定時点認識（完成基準）の建設契約では、現金回収時でも工事請求額が帳簿原価を超えた時点でもなく、契約完成時のコントロール移転をもって収益を認識する。現金回収および請求のマイルストーンは契約資産・負債の貸借対照表表示には影響するが、完成基準の収益認識タイミングとは無関係である。

questions.subtopic: Measure of Progress for Over-Time Revenue Recognition
questions.stem: Acorn Software provides subscribers with access to web-based software for 365 days for a single payment, regardless of how often the customer uses the software. Acorn developed the software, regularly updates it, and provides 24-hour customer service access for subscribers at no extra charge. If the company recognizes revenue while the performance obligation is being satisfied, what base is most appropriate to measure progress toward completion of the obligation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Customer's monthly usage.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Labor hours expended.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Subscription time elapsed.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Subscriptions sold annually.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-31 and 606-10-25-33, the measure of progress should faithfully depict the entity's performance. The subscription grants continuous access to the software for 365 days regardless of usage; accordingly, the performance obligation is a stand-ready obligation satisfied evenly over time. Subscription time elapsed (straight-line over 365 days) best reflects this pattern. Customer usage is not relevant because the contract price does not vary with usage.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-31および606-10-25-33により、進捗の測定方法はエンティティの履行を忠実に描写するものでなければならない。サブスクリプションは利用頻度にかかわらず365日間のアクセスを付与するため、履行義務は一定期間にわたって均等に充足される「待機義務」である。経過したサブスクリプション期間（365日間の均等配分）が最も適切な測定基準となる。顧客の利用量は契約価格と連動しないため不適切。

questions.subtopic: IP License Classification — Right to Access vs. Right to Use
questions.stem: Paw Gallery and Shell Portraits both offer downloadable photographs to their customers. Paw Gallery has a collection of animal photographs that may be downloaded by its subscribers. Subscribers pay an annual fee for an a ccess code allowing the user to download images from the site. Shell Portraits takes photographs at the customer's location. Shell emails each customer a unique code to access their photos once they are completed. Each access code is active for one week after it has been delivered. Both Paw and Shell allow unlimited reproduction of their images. When should Paw and Shell recognize revenue for these image sales? Paw Gallery Shell Portraits

choices.label: A
choices.text: At access code delivery At access code delivery

choices.label: B
choices.text: At access code delivery Over access duration

choices.label: C
choices.text: Over access duration At access code delivery

choices.label: D
choices.text: Over access duration Over access duration

questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-58 through 55-60, intellectual property licenses are classified as either a 'right to access' (over time) or a 'right to use' (point in time). Paw Gallery provides ongoing access to a changing collection (it likely updates the catalog), constituting a right to access — revenue over the subscription duration. Shell Portraits licenses specific photographs that are complete and unchanging; the code is merely the delivery mechanism for a one-time transfer of a specific asset, constituting a right to use — revenue at code delivery.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-58〜55-60により、知的財産のライセンスは「アクセス権」（一定期間）または「使用権」（特定時点）に分類される。Paw Galleryは変化する写真コレクションへの継続的アクセスを提供しており（カタログの更新が見込まれる）、アクセス権に該当→サブスクリプション期間にわたって収益認識。Shell Portraitsは特定の完成した写真をライセンスし、コードは一回限りの資産移転の手段であり使用権に該当→コード配信時点で収益認識。

questions.subtopic: Transaction Price Allocation — Material Rights and Auto-Renewal Options
questions.stem: A company sells scented beauty products and personal care items. It is currently offering a promotion: customers can purchase a deluxe beauty kit for $500, which is its normal retail price, and get one month's free access to an online beauty consultant, which normally costs $40. To get the free access, customers must agree to a default auto - renewal of the service, cancellable at any time. The company estimates that the auto - renewal option will result in customers, on average, opting for six months of paid access after the free month expires. For each deluxe beauty kit sold as part of this promotion, how m uch revenue should be allocated to each performance obligation and how much deferred revenue should the company recognize? Beauty kit Beauty consultant Deferred revenue
questions.explanation: The transaction price is $500. The performance obligations are the beauty kit ($500 standalone) and one month of consultant access ($40 standalone). The auto-renewal option at market price is not a material right (customers pay the standard rate for subsequent months), so it does not create a separate performance obligation or deferred revenue. Total standalone prices = $500 + $40 = $540. Beauty kit allocation = $500/$540 × $500 ≈ $462.96 ≈ $463; consultant allocation = $40/$540 × $500 ≈ $37.04 ≈ $37. Deferred revenue = $0 because no material right exists.
questions.explanationJa: 取引価格は$500。履行義務は美容キット（独立価格$500）と1か月間のコンサルタントアクセス（独立価格$40）の2つ。自動更新オプションは市場価格での更新であるため重要な権利（マテリアル・ライト）に該当せず、追加の履行義務や繰延収益は発生しない。独立販売価格合計＝$540。美容キット配分＝$500÷$540×$500≈$463、コンサルタント配分≈$37。繰延収益＝$0。

questions.subtopic: Cost-to-Cost Method — Percentage of Completion Ratio
questions.stem: The calculation of income in Year 3 of a five - year construction contract accounted for using the cost - to - cost method includes the ratio of

choices.label: A
choices.text: Costs incurred in Year 3 to total billings.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Costs incurred in Year 3 to total estimated costs.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total costs incurred to date to total billings.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Total costs incurred to date to total estimated costs.

questions.explanation: Under the cost-to-cost method per ASC 606-10-25-31, the percentage of completion is computed as cumulative costs incurred to date divided by total estimated costs (cumulative costs incurred to date plus estimated costs to complete). This cumulative-to-date ratio is then applied to total expected profit to determine cumulative income earned, from which previously recognized income is subtracted to get the current year amount. Billings do not appear in this ratio.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-31のコスト法では、進捗率＝累計発生原価（期末時点）÷総見積原価（累計発生原価＋残余見積原価）。この累計比率を総見積利益に乗じて累計認識利益を求め、既認識利益を控除して当期の利益を算出する。請求額（工事請求）はこの比率の計算には使用しない。

questions.stem: Fenn Stores, Inc. had sales of $1,000,000 during December, Year 2. Experience has shown that merchandise equaling 7% of sales will be returned within 30 days and that an additional 3% will be returned within 90 days. Returned merchandise is readily resalable . In addition, merchandise equaling 15% of sales will be exchanged for merchandise of equal or greater value. What amount should Fenn report for net sales in its income statement for the month of December, Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-55-22 and 55-23, revenue is reduced for expected returns (refund of consideration). Expected returns = 7% + 3% = 10% of $1,000,000 = $100,000. Net sales = $1,000,000 − $100,000 = $900,000. Exchanges for equal or greater value are not returns; they do not reduce revenue because the entity retains equivalent consideration. The 15% exchange rate has no impact on net sales.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-22および55-23により、収益は返品見込み分だけ減額する。返品見込み＝7%＋3%＝10%×$1,000,000＝$100,000。純売上＝$1,000,000－$100,000＝$900,000。同等以上の価値への交換は返品ではなく、対価を保持するため収益を減らさない。15%の交換は純売上に影響しない。

questions.subtopic: Revenue Recognition — Collectability and Nonrefundable Consideration
questions.stem: Bareb Co. sells 20 televisions to a new customer for $20,000. The customer is opening a sports bar, and Bareb is aware the sports bar will be subject to intense competition. Upon delivery, a $5,000 nonrefundable down payment is collected a nd the remaining $15,000 is due evenly over the next two years. If the customer defaults, Bareb's only recourse is to repossess the televisions. Bareb is uncertain as to the collectability of the remaining payments. What amount of revenue, if any, shoul d Bareb recognize upon transfer of the televisions?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 606-10-25-7(e), collection of the consideration must be probable for revenue recognition. Since Bareb is uncertain about the $15,000 balance and has only repossession rights, the $15,000 does not meet the collectability threshold. However, per ASC 606-10-25-7 and 606-10-25-9, when collectability is not probable, an entity recognizes revenue only to the extent it has received nonrefundable consideration and has no remaining obligation to transfer goods. The televisions have been delivered and the $5,000 is nonrefundable, so $5,000 of revenue is recognized upon delivery.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-25-7(e)により、収益認識には対価の回収可能性が高いことが必要。Barebは残高$15,000の回収可能性に不確実性があり、唯一の救済措置がテレビの再取得のみであるため、$15,000は回収可能性の基準を満たさない。ただし、ASC 606-10-25-7および25-9により、回収可能性が高くない場合でも、返金不要の対価を受領し追加の財・サービス移転義務がない範囲で収益認識できる。テレビは引き渡し済みで$5,000は返金不要のため、引渡し時に$5,000の収益を認識する。

questions.subtopic: Right of Return — Operating Profit Calculation
questions.stem: Lew Co. sold 200,000 corrugated boxes for $2 each. Lew's cost was $1 per unit. The sales agreement gave the customer the right to return up to 60% of the boxes withi n the first six months, provided an appropriate reason was given. It was immediately determined, with appropriate reason, that 5% of the boxes would be returned. Lew absorbed an additional $10,000 to process the returns and expects to resell the boxes. Wh at amount should Lew report as operating profit from this transaction?
questions.explanation: Expected returns = 5% × 200,000 = 10,000 boxes. Net units sold = 190,000. Net revenue = 190,000 × $2 = $380,000. Cost of goods sold for 190,000 units = 190,000 × $1 = $190,000. Right to recover returned goods (asset) = 10,000 × $1 = $10,000 (offsets refund liability at cost). Return processing cost = $10,000 (expensed). Operating profit = $380,000 revenue − $190,000 COGS − $10,000 processing = $180,000. The right-to-recover asset and refund liability net to zero impact on profit.

questions.subtopic: Point-in-Time Recognition — Anticipated Loss on Onerous Contract
questions.stem:
The following data pertains to a company's long-term contracts, which commenced during Year 2:

| | Project 1 | Project 2 |
|---|---:|---:|
| Contract price | $420,000 | $300,000 |
| Costs incurred during Year 2 | $240,000 | $280,000 |
| Estimated costs to complete | $120,000 | $40,000 |
| Billed to customers during Year 2 | $150,000 | $270,000 |
| Received from customers during Year 2 | $90,000 | $250,000 |

If the company recognizes revenues and costs from contracts at a point in time, what amount of gross profit (loss) would the company report in its Year 2 income statement?
questions.explanation: Under point-in-time recognition, revenue on Project 1 and Project 2 is deferred until completion — neither was completed in Year 2, so no revenue or cost is recognized for either project. However, when a contract becomes a loss contract, the loss must be recognized immediately per ASC 606-10-25-7. Project 2 total estimated cost = $280,000 + $40,000 = $320,000 vs. contract price $300,000 → anticipated loss = $20,000. Project 1 shows a profit ($420,000 price vs. $360,000 total cost), so no loss is recognized. Year 2 gross profit (loss) = $(20,000).
questions.explanationJa: 特定時点認識では、Project 1・Project 2ともに完成まで収益認識を繰り延べる。第2年度には両プロジェクトとも完成していないため収益・費用は認識しない。ただし、ASC 606-10-25-7により損失契約は即時認識が必要。Project 2の総見積原価＝$280,000＋$40,000＝$320,000 vs. 契約金額$300,000 → 損失$20,000。Project 1は利益契約（$420,000 vs. $360,000）なので損失なし。第2年度の粗利益（損失）＝$(20,000)。

questions.subtopic: Interim Reporting — Property Taxes and Unanticipated Repairs
questions.stem: On January 16, Tree Co. paid $60,000 in property taxes on its factory for the current calendar year. On April 2, Tree paid $240,000 for unanticipated major repairs to its factory equipment. The repairs will benefit operations for the remainder of the calendar year. What amount of these expenses should Tree include in its third quarter interim financial statements for the three months ended September 30?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 270-10-45-10, costs and expenses that clearly benefit more than one interim period are allocated to those periods based on estimated time expired, benefit received, or activity associated with the periods, rather than expensed entirely when paid. Property taxes benefit the full year and are accrued ratably: Q3 share = 3/12 × $60,000 = $15,000. The major repairs, paid April 2, explicitly benefit operations for the remainder of the calendar year (April through December, 9 months), so the cost is allocated over those 9 months: Q3 share = 3/9 × $240,000 = $80,000. Total interim expense for the three months ended September 30 = $15,000 + $80,000 = $95,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 270-10-45-10により、複数の中間期間に便益をもたらすことが明らかな費用は、発生時に全額費用化するのではなく、経過期間・便益・活動実態に基づき各期間へ配分する。固定資産税は年間を通じて便益をもたらすため均等按分：第3四半期分＝3/12×$60,000＝$15,000。4月2日に支払った重大な修繕費は「年内の残り期間（4月〜12月の9か月間）に便益をもたらす」と明記されているため、この9か月間に配分する：第3四半期分＝3/9×$240,000＝$80,000。9月30日に終わる3か月間の中間費用合計＝$15,000＋$80,000＝$95,000。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Munn agreed to sell the assets and product line of one of its operating divisions for $1,600,000, which was expected to result in a gain on disposition. The sale was consummated on December 31, Year 2, and resulted in a gain on disposition of $450,000. This division's net operating losses were $250,000 in Year 1 and $320,000 in Year 2. The income tax rate for both years was 30%. In preparing its income statements, Munn should report which of the following amounts of gain (loss) from discontinued operations? Year 1 Year 2
questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10 and ASC 205-20, discontinued operations include operating results (net of tax) each year and gain/loss on disposal in the year of actual sale. Year 1: operating loss = $250,000 × (1 − 30%) = $175,000 after-tax loss = $(175,000). A gain was anticipated in Year 1 but is not recognized until realized; the expected gain does not offset the Year 1 loss. Year 2: operating loss $(320,000) net of tax = $(224,000); gain on disposal $450,000 net of tax = $315,000; net Year 2 discontinued operations = $315,000 − $224,000 = $91,000.

questions.stem: In financial reporting of segment data, which of the following would be used to determine a segment's operating income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain or loss on discontinued operations.

choices.label: B
choices.text: General corporate expenses.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Intersegment sales.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Income tax expense.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 280-10-50-22, a segment's operating profit or loss is computed as revenue (including intersegment revenue) minus segment-level expenses. Intersegment sales are included in the measure of segment revenue used to evaluate performance. In contrast, gains or losses on discontinued operations, general corporate expenses, and income tax expense are not allocated to individual segments and are excluded from segment operating income.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 280-10-50-22に基づき、セグメントの営業損益はセグメント間売上を含む収益からセグメントレベルの費用を控除して算定します。セグメント間売上はセグメント収益の評価指標に含まれます。一方、非継続事業の損益、一般本社費用、法人税費用はセグメントに配賦せず、セグメント営業利益の計算から除外されます。

questions.stem: In financial reporting of segment data, which of the following must be considered to determine whether an industry segment is a reportable segment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Yes, (2) Yes

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) Yes, (2) No

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) No, (2) Yes

questions.explanation: Under ASC 280-10-50-12, a segment is reportable if its revenue (including both external sales to unaffiliated customers and intersegment sales) is 10% or more of the combined revenues of all operating segments. Both types of revenue must be considered in applying the 10% revenue threshold test for identifying reportable segments.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 280-10-50-12に基づき、セグメントは外部売上とセグメント間売上の両方を含む収益が、全セグメントの合計収益の10%以上であれば報告対象セグメントとなります。10%収益閾値テストの適用において、両種類の収益を考慮する必要があります。

questions.stem: On December 1, Year 1, Pinn Corp., a calendar-year corporation, paid $40,000 for insurance to be used in calendar Year 2. On July 3, Year 2, Pinn prepaid $430,000 for advertising services. The advertising services will be provided over the remainder of the calendar year. What amount of these expenses should Pinn include in its September 30, Year 2 interim financial statements?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 270-10-45-9, costs that benefit two or more interim periods should be allocated among those periods. The $40,000 insurance covers all of Year 2 (4 quarters), so Q3 (July–September) receives $40,000 ÷ 4 = $10,000. The $430,000 advertising is paid July 3 and benefits the remainder of Year 2 (July through December = 6 months); Q3 covers 3 of those 6 months, so Q3 receives $430,000 × 3/6 = $215,000. Total Q3 expense = $10,000 + $215,000 = $225,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 270-10-45-9に基づき、複数の中間期間に恩恵をもたらすコストはそれらの期間に配賦します。保険料$40,000はYear 2全体（4四半期）に対するもので、Q3の取り分は$40,000÷4=$10,000です。広告費$430,000は7月3日払いでYear 2の残余期間（7月〜12月=6ヶ月）に恩恵をもたらし、Q3はその3/6を受け取るため$430,000×3/6=$215,000となります。Q3合計=$10,000+$215,000=$225,000。

questions.stem: On December 31, Year 1, Greer Co. entered into an agreement to sell its Hart segment's assets. On that date, Greer estimated the gain from the disposition of the assets in Year 2 would be $700,000. Hart's actual operating losses were $200,000 in Year 1 and $300,000 in Year 2. The actual gain on disposition of Hart's assets in Year 2 was $650,000. Disregarding income taxes, what net gain (loss) should be reported for discontinued operations in Greer's Year 1 and Year 2 income statements? Year 1 Year 2
